crt_sh_data.issuer_name: Let's Encrypt
Let's Encrypt

narrative.executive_summary: Development Mode: Simulated threat intelligence brief for acme-supplier.com. The external network footprint shows standard HTTP/S port availability and low reputation risk.

top_indicators.title: Exposed Port 22 (SSH)
top_indicators.detail: SSH daemon is listening on public interface.

top_indicators.title: Let's Encrypt TLS Burst
top_indicators.detail: Active SSL certificates created within a 48 hour window.

top_indicators.title: VirusTotal Clean
top_indicators.detail: No malicious reputation logs detected for this domain.

recommended_actions: Restrict ingress firewall rules on port 22.
Verify domain ownership certificate registries.
Establish continuous OSINT security monitoring.

narrative.executive_summary: Development Mode: Simulated threat intelligence brief for stripe.com. The external network footprint shows standard HTTP/S port availability and low reputation risk.

top_indicators.title: Exposed Port 22 (SSH)
top_indicators.detail: SSH daemon is listening on public interface.

top_indicators.title: Let's Encrypt TLS Burst
top_indicators.detail: Active SSL certificates created within a 48 hour window.

top_indicators.title: VirusTotal Clean
top_indicators.detail: No malicious reputation logs detected for this domain.

recommended_actions: Restrict ingress firewall rules on port 22.
Verify domain ownership certificate registries.
Establish continuous OSINT security monitoring.

scan_metadata.explanation: Initial scan baseline established. Exposed surface includes 0 open ports and 0 vulnerabilities.

narrative.executive_summary: The threat intelligence briefing for keinscbse.com reveals a moderately complex external attack surface footprint. Standard web assets are online, but port mapping indicates database and administrative protocols are public-facing, exposing critical corporate data. Direct mitigation is recommended within the standard SLA period.
narrative.analyst_summary: Detailed technical analysis identified open ports 22 (SSH) and 5432 (PostgreSQL) active on public IP addresses. In addition, SSL Transparency Logs indicate Let's Encrypt certificates were generated in a short burst, which is a classic indicator of infrastructure staging. Vulnerability scanning flagged CVE-2023-3519 with high exploit scores.
narrative.compliance_summary: Observed vulnerabilities and credential exposures violate NIST CSF PR.IP-1 (Access control lists updated) and SOC 2 CC6.1 (Logical access controls to database assets). Core changes are required for compliance readiness.
recommended_actions: Restrict public access on Ports 22 and 5432 using a strict network security firewall.
Rotate all private API keys and tokens exposed in indexed GitHub public repositories.
Patch Citrix ADC systems to remediate critical CVE-2023-3519 vulnerability.
Configure standard DNSSEC and SPF/DKIM validation records across all subdomains.

top_indicators.title: Public Database Exposure (PostgreSQL)
top_indicators.detail: Active SQL database on Port 5432 exposed without perimeter authentication.

top_indicators.title: CISA KEV Exploit Path (CVE-2023-3519)
top_indicators.detail: Active Citrix Gateway vulnerability with high EPSS probability detected on host.

top_indicators.title: GitHub API Token Leak
top_indicators.detail: Accidental code repository upload containing private domain secrets and SaaS passwords.

narrative.benchmark_summary: This vendor exhibits a risk score of 48, putting them in the 55th percentile, which represents higher risk than 45% of peer SaaS suppliers.
narrative.historical_trend_summary: Postures have declined over the past 30 days, showing an attack surface growth of 12% due to new port activations.
narrative.threat_similarity_summary: Vector fingerprint analysis correlation shows moderate overlap (74%) with Cozy Bear (APT29) staging methods.
narrative.risk_forecasting_summary: Based on active telemetry, there is a 65% probability of a secondary security event or credential leak within the next 90 days.

You agree that you will use this data only for lawful purposes and will not to use this data to allow, enable, or otherwise make possible, dissemination or collection of this data in part or in its entirety for any purpose such as the transmission of unsolicited advertising and solicitations of any kind, including spam. You further agree not to use this data to enable high volume, automated or robotic electronic processes designed to collect or compile this data for any purpose, including mining this data for your own personal or commercial purposes, or use this data in any way that violates applicable laws and regulations. We reserve the right to restrict or deny your access to this service if we suspect that you have failed to comply with these terms. We reserve the right to modify this agreement at any time.

scan_metadata.explanation: Initial scan baseline established. Exposed surface includes 0 open ports and 0 vulnerabilities.

crt_sh_data.issuer_name: Let's Encrypt

threat_signatures.description: Russian SVR-linked group. Favors typo-squatted SaaS domains, Let's Encrypt certs issued in bursts, and supply chain compromise via SolarWinds-style update channels.
Chinese state-sponsored group blending espionage and financially-motivated intrusions. Known for trojanized installers and abuse of code-signing certificates.
North Korean threat actor. Notable for cryptocurrency exchange compromise and supply chain attacks via npm/PyPI package squatting.
Financially-motivated cybercrime group targeting retail/hospitality. Uses spear-phished invoices and cloned vendor portals with valid TLS certs.
GRU Unit 74455. Targets critical infrastructure and software supply chains (NotPetya). Watches for exposed RDP, SMB, and ICS protocols.
